title: edit: A recent systematic literature review conducted by Marques and Ferreira (2020) highlighted a sharp increase in the…

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: edit: A recent systematic literature review conducted by Marques and Ferreira (2020) highlighted a sharp increase in the last twenty years of studies on the topic of digital transformation in healthcare originations such as hospitals.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: Edited: Marques and Ferreira (2020) conducted a recent systematic literature review that revealed a significant surge in research on digital transformation in healthcare organizations, particularly hospitals, in the last 20 years.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: Revised: In their 2020 systematic literature review, Marques and Ferreira found a notable rise in research on the subject of digital transformation in healthcare institutions such as hospitals over the past two decades.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: improve : Despite the increasing importance of digital transformation in healthcare systems , the journey towards digital transformation of hospitals still presents challenges(Raimo et al., 2023) with most digital transformation projects falling short of expectations (Preko and Boateng, 2020). A recent systematic literature review conducted by Marques and Ferreira (2020) highlighted a sharp increase in the last twenty years of studies on the topic of digital transformation in healthcare originations such as hospitals. However, the authors highlighted that these studies focused mainly on specific issues such as integrated management, medical images, electronic medical records and virtual services. The contributions that dealt with the digital transformation of hospitals from a broader perspective focused mainly on the identification of the different digital technologies implemented, on the advantages and benefits deriving from the implementation of digital technologies and on the barriers and challenges associated with the implementation of these technologies (Tortorella et al., 2020a). However, there is an important gap in the academic literature since only a limited number of studies have examined the drivers capable of influencing the level of digital transformation of hospitals, so empirical research on the drivers of digital transformation of hospitals is still lacking (Raimo et al., 2023; Kraus et al., 2021b).

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: Improved: Despite the growing significance of digital transformation in healthcare systems, the transition of hospitals towards digitalization continues to encounter challenges (Raimo et al., 2023), with many projects failing to meet expectations (Preko and Boateng, 2020). Although a recent systematic literature review by Marques and Ferreira (2020) revealed an increase in studies on digital transformation in healthcare organizations, the focus of these studies was primarily on specific issues such as electronic medical records, medical images, virtual services, and integrated management. The few contributions that addressed the broader aspects of the digital transformation of hospitals emphasized the identification of implemented digital technologies, benefits deriving from implementation, and challenges associated with digital technology adoption (Tortorella et al., 2020a). However, the academic literature suffers from an important gap, as only a limited number of studies have investigated drivers that shape the degree of digital transformation of hospitals. As a result, empirical research on the drivers of hospital digital transformation remains scarce (Raimo et al., 2023; Kraus et al., 2021b).

Although a recent systematic literature review by Marques and Ferreira (2020) revealed an increase in studies on digital transformation in healthcare organizations, , especially hospitals, the focus of these studies was primarily on specific issues such as electronic medical records, medical images, virtual services, and integrated management. The few contributions that addressed the broader aspects of the digital transformation of hospitals emphasized the identification of implemented digital technologies, benefits deriving from implementation, and challenges associated with digital technology implementation (Tortorella et al., 2020a). However, there is an important gap in the academic literature since only a limited number of studies have examined the drivers capable of influencing the level of digital transformation of hospitals, so empirical research on the drivers of digital transformation of hospitals is still lacking (Raimo et al., 2023; Kraus et al., 2021b).

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: Edited: Despite the growing significance of digital transformation in healthcare systems, the digital transformation journey of hospitals continues to face challenges (Raimo et al., 2023), with numerous digital transformation projects falling short of expectations (Preko and Boateng, 2020). While Marques and Ferreira's (2020) systematic literature review showed a rise in studies on digital transformation in healthcare organizations, mainly hospitals, the studies primarily focused on specific aspects such as electronic medical records, medical images, virtual services, and integrated management. Although a few contributions addressed the broader aspects of hospital digital transformation, focusing on the identification of digital technologies implemented, benefits and challenges associated with implementation, academic literature lacks empirical research on drivers that influence digital transformation of hospitals (Raimo et al., 2023; Kraus et al., 2021b).
